mission.title: Install and Configure Flowise
mission.description: Flowise is an AI platform that allows users to build AI agents visually. It is ideal for developers looking to create and manage AI workflows without extensive coding. Key trade-offs include the need for Node.js and Docker for installation and potential resource requirements for running complex AI models.

steps.title: Install the Flowise CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Flowise command-line interface globally on your machine, which is necessary for deploying Flowise on Kubernetes.

```bash

npm install -g flowise@3.0.13

```

> Install the specific version of Flowise CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create flowise-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials flowise-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command fetches the cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy Flowise on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys Flowise on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add flowise https://flowiseai.github.io/Flowise

helm install flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise --create-namespace

```

> Add the Flowise Helm repository.
> Install Flowise in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Flowise deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment flowise --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace flowise

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml --namespace flowise

```

> Set up autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description:
Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources allocated.

```bash

gcloud compute instances list

```

> Check your running instances and their costs.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Flowise
uninstall.description:
This will remove the Flowise deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if persistent volumes are not handled properly.

```bash

helm uninstall flowise --namespace flowise

gcloud container clusters delete flowise-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Uninstall Flowise from the specified namespace.
> Delete the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Flowise
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step will upgrade Flowise to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise

kubectl get pods --namespace flowise

```

> Cordon and drain the node to safely upgrade.
> Upgrade Flowise to the specified version.
> Verify that the Flowise pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<PORT>: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable, possibly due to network issues or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig and ensure the cluster is running with 'gcloud container clusters list'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: release flowise not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Flowise release has not been installed in the specified namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure you have installed Flowise using the correct namespace and check with 'helm list --namespace flowise'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the application is failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace flowise' and verify your configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources allocated to meet the demands of the Flowise deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale up your nodes or adjust the resource requests/limits in your deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved UI for agent workflows.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy API endpoints for agent creation.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.x, ensure to update your environment variables and configurations as per the new documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods --namespace flowise' showing all pods in 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
